name: saju-mvp
description: 사주팔자 앱 MVP 구현 - Expo + Hono + manseryeok 기반

members.name: frontend-dev
members.prompt:
You are the frontend developer for the saju-mvp team. Your task is Task #4: Build mobile app screens and UI components.

Working directory: /Users/kangnam/projects/saju/

**IMPORTANT**: The project uses Expo SDK 55, Expo Router (file-based routing), NativeWind v4 (Tailwind CSS for React Native), Zustand + MMKV, and React Native Reanimated. All are already installed.

NativeWind is configured:
- metro.config.js wraps with `withNativeWind`
- global.css has @tailwind directives
- tailwind.config.ts is set up with content paths and 오행 custom colors

The existing template files in app/ need to be replaced with our screens.

**Key Design System:**
- Dark theme: bg-slate-950 (main bg), bg-slate-900 (cards), text-amber-100 (primary text), text-slate-300 (secondary text)
- 오행 colors: wood=#10b981 (emerald), fire=#f43f5e (rose), earth=#f59e0b (amber), metal=#e2e8f0 (slate-200), water=#3b82f6 (blue)
- Card-centric UI with rounded-2xl, subtle borders (border-slate-800)
- Use `className` prop with NativeWind (NOT StyleSheet)

**IMPORTANT NativeWind v4 Notes:**
- Use `className` prop on React Native components (View, Text, TouchableOpacity, etc.)
- Import from 'react-native' as usual, NativeWind processes className at build time
- The `cssInterop` is NOT needed for standard RN components in NativeWind v4 with the preset

Create these files:

---

### ROOT LAYOUT: app/_layout.tsx
```tsx
import '../global.css';
import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
import { StatusBar } from 'expo-status-bar';

export default function RootLayout() {
  return (
    <>
      <StatusBar style="light" />
      <Stack screenOptions={{
        headerStyle: { backgroundColor: '#0f172a' },
        headerTintColor: '#fef3c7',
        headerTitleStyle: { fontWeight: 'bold' },
        contentStyle: { backgroundColor: '#020617' },
      }}>
        <Stack.Screen name="(tabs)" options={{ headerShown: false }} />
        <Stack.Screen name="saju/input" options={{ title: '사주 입력', presentation: 'modal' }} />
        <Stack.Screen name="saju/result" options={{ title: '사주 결과' }} />
        <Stack.Screen name="gunghap/input" options={{ title: '궁합 입력', presentation: 'modal' }} />
        <Stack.Screen name="gunghap/result" options={{ title: '궁합 결과' }} />
        <Stack.Screen name="fortune/daily" options={{ title: '오늘의 운세' }} />
      </Stack>
    </>
  );
}
```

### TAB LAYOUT: app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx
4 tabs: 홈, 사주, 궁합, 내 정보
- Use Expo Router Tabs component
- Dark theme tab bar (bg-slate-950, active tint amber-400)
- Icons: use simple emoji or text for now (we'll add proper icons later)
- Tab labels: 홈, 사주, 궁합, 프로필

### TAB SCREENS:

**app/(tabs)/index.tsx** - Home tab
- Hero section: "오늘의 운세" with date display
- Quick fortune summary card (placeholder scores)
- Quick action buttons: "내 사주 보기", "궁합 보기", "오늘의 운세 상세"
- Each button navigates using router.push()

**app/(tabs)/saju.tsx** - Saju hub tab
- Title: "사주팔자"
- Description text explaining what saju reading is
- Big CTA button: "사주 보기" → navigates to /saju/input
- History section (placeholder for future saved readings)

**app/(tabs)/gunghap.tsx** - Gunghap hub tab
- Title: "궁합"
- Description text
- CTA button → navigates to /gunghap/input
- Saved matches section (placeholder)

**app/(tabs)/profile.tsx** - Profile tab
- Title: "내 정보"
- Birth info form (display/edit)
- Saved persons list (placeholder)
- App settings section

### STACK SCREENS:

**app/saju/input.tsx** - Birth Input
- Form fields:
  - Year/Month/Day with TextInput (numeric keyboard)
  - Hour/Minute with TextInput (optional, with "시간 모름" toggle)
  - Gender selector (M/F buttons)
  - Calendar type toggle (양력/음력)
  - Leap month toggle (only shows when 음력 selected)
- "사주 보기" submit button
- On submit: navigate to /saju/result with params

**app/saju/result.tsx** - Saju Result
- Receives birth params
- Calls calculateFullSaju() from src/lib/saju/calculator
- Displays FourPillarsCard component
- Displays FiveElementsChart component
- AI interpretation section with streaming text
- Import types from src/lib/saju/types

**app/gunghap/input.tsx** - Two Person Input
- Two sets of birth input forms (Person A, Person B)
- Person names
- Submit button

**app/gunghap/result.tsx** - Compatibility Result
- Displays both persons' pillars
- Compatibility score (animated)
- AI interpretation streaming

**app/fortune/daily.tsx** - Daily Fortune Detail
- Today's pillar display
- Category scores (overall, love, career, wealth, health)
- Lucky info (color, number, direction)
- AI interpretation

### Delete unnecessary template files:
- Remove app/(tabs)/two.tsx, app/modal.tsx, app/+html.tsx
- Remove components/EditScreenInfo.tsx, components/ExternalLink.tsx, components/StyledText.tsx, components/Themed.tsx
- Keep app/+not-found.tsx but update it with dark theme

---

### UI COMPONENTS:

**src/components/ui/Button.tsx**
```tsx
// Variants: primary (amber bg), secondary (slate bg), outline
// Props: title, onPress, variant, disabled, loading
// Dark theme styled with NativeWind className
```

**src/components/ui/Card.tsx**
```tsx
// Dark card container: bg-slate-900, rounded-2xl, border border-slate-800, p-4
// Props: children, className (for additional styles)
```

**src/components/ui/Input.tsx**
```tsx
// Styled TextInput: bg-slate-800, text-slate-100, rounded-xl, placeholder-slate-500
// Props: label, error, + standard TextInput props
```

**src/components/saju/FourPillarsCard.tsx**
```tsx
// 4-column layout showing 사주팔자
// Each column: pillar name (년주/월주/일주/시주), 천간, 지지
// Color-coded by element
// Props: fourPillars: FourPillars
```

**src/components/saju/FiveElementsChart.tsx**
```tsx
// Horizontal bar chart showing 오행 distribution
// Each element with its color and count/percentage
// Props: analysis: FiveElementsAnalysis
```

**src/components/saju/StreamingText.tsx**
```tsx
// Displays AI interpretation text with typing animation
// Props: text: string, isStreaming: boolean
// Shows cursor animation while streaming
```

**src/components/gunghap/CompatibilityScore.tsx**
```tsx
// Animated circular score display
// Large number in center with ring progress
// Props: score: number, label: string
```

**src/components/fortune/DailyFortuneCard.tsx**
```tsx
// Summary card for daily fortune
// Shows overall score, key highlights
// Props: fortune: DailyFortune
```

---

### STORES (src/stores/):

**src/stores/userStore.ts**
```ts
import { create } from 'zustand';
// User profile store with MMKV persistence
// Fields: birthDate, birthTime, gender, calendarType, isLeapMonth, displayName
// Actions: setProfile, clearProfile
// Note: For MMKV persistence, just use zustand's persist middleware with a custom MMKV storage adapter
// But for simplicity in MVP, use plain zustand without persistence first
```

**src/stores/sajuStore.ts**
```ts
// Recent saju calculations cache
// Fields: lastResult: SajuAnalysis | null, lastInterpretation: string
// Actions: setResult, setInterpretation, clear
```

---

### HOOKS (src/hooks/):

**src/hooks/useSajuCalculation.ts**
```ts
import { calculateFullSaju } from '@/src/lib/saju/calculator';
// Hook that wraps calculateFullSaju with loading state
// Returns: { calculate, result, isLoading, error }
```

**src/hooks/useInterpretation.ts**
```ts
// Hook for SSE streaming from backend
// Takes endpoint URL and request data
// Returns: { interpret, text, isStreaming, error }
// Handles EventSource/fetch with streaming
```

### API CLIENT (src/lib/api/):

**src/lib/api/client.ts**
```ts
const API_BASE = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';

export async function fetchSSE(endpoint: string, body: any, onChunk: (text: string) => void): Promise<void> {
  // POST request with fetch
  // Read response body as stream
  // Parse SSE events: data: {"text": "..."}\n\n
  // Call onChunk for each text chunk
  // Handle [DONE] event
}
```

**src/lib/api/interpretation.ts**
```ts
export async function interpretSaju(sajuData: SajuAnalysis, onChunk: (text: string) => void): Promise<void>
export async function interpretGunghap(personA: SajuAnalysis, personB: SajuAnalysis, onChunk: (text: string) => void): Promise<void>
export async function interpretDaily(dayMaster: any, todayPillar: any, onChunk: (text: string) => void): Promise<void>
```

---

### IMPORTANT NOTES:
1. For the saju/result.tsx screen, the calculation types come from src/lib/saju/types.ts. Import them from there. The actual calculation function may not be ready yet, so use a try/catch and show a placeholder if it fails.
2. Use `useLocalSearchParams()` from expo-router to get navigation params.
3. All screens should have ScrollView for scrollable content.
4. Use SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context.
5. For the tab bar icons, use simple Unicode characters or text labels (e.g., '🏠', '🔮', '💑', '👤')

Mark task #4 as in_progress when starting, and completed when done.
